title: split remote tests from distro tests

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I looked at the failing tests...curious there's a mix of failures across regular and remote.  Needs deeper looking...

One of the remote tests (f28) I see this:

```
[+0134s] Error: could not create runtime: dial unix /run/podman/io.podman-7c1078a93a645fca368d4090d2fb3e5038441fc6c5bc2ab67685759f4d53a938: connect: no such file or directory
```

Do you need to set `$PODMAN_VARLINK_ADDRESS` or should the automatic value work?

The podman-in-podman (F29) is an interesting failure in `Podman create with --ip flag `:

```
[+0222s] Error: unable to start container "test1": error configuring network namespace for container 63b006b712929a92675708291a6041aca6000718c3166410cebc7eaa18c5346d: failed to allocate for range 0: requested IP address 10.88.64.128 is not available in range set 10.88.0.1-10.88.255.254
```

Clearly that address is valid, could another parallel test have grabbed?  In any case, this seems like something that should be retried (i.e. with another address) by some lower-level code (not the test), no?  (should I open an issue on this?)

The other failures in that run all show something similar to this:

```
[+0822s] Error: error removing pod a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b conmon cgroup: cgroups: unable to remove paths /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/freezer/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/pids/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/net_cls/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/net_prio/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/perf_event/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuacct/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/memory/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/blkio/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/devices/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon, /sys/fs/cgroup/hugetlb/libpod_parent/a9a8d67ce07adbd2773b44b8e6f2ce6c0b0a06eabc16c083c61899fd0870164b/conmon
```
The IP test, to the best of my knowledge, creates a custom CNI network and allocates inside it. It looks like Podman isn't finding said CNI network - race condition, or maybe a setup error?

> or maybe a setup error?

That's possible, though I would think should show more consistent failures for that test.  Installing the CNI stuff happens at image-build time (until #2561  merges).  But since this is podman-in-podman, maybe some additional steps are done/required?
@cevich I mean that test in particular installs a separate CNI network to use (just for the one test)

Ahh, hmmm, okay, so perhaps that run was simply flaky and we can just stick our heads back into the warm, cozy, soft, sand :grinning:    Those errors don't seem like that could be related to what Brent's doing here.

> This is great, we really need these changes and I'm glad to see you got them working. I think we need a similar change here:
> 
> https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/rootless_test.sh#L24-L25
> 
> Also would be good to update the docs for this `.cirrus.yml` testing task:
> 
> https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/README.md#testing-task

the rootless stuff will come in a different PR ... I want to keep the changes to a minimum on each step.

For `rootless_test.sh` to work, the new env. var will need to get passed into the user's environment.  Perhaps by jamming it into their `$ENVLIB` (aka `.bash_profile`) during that part of the setup: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh#L195-L204

> the rootless stuff will come in a different PR ... I want to keep the changes to a minimum on each step.

Oh ok, that's fine.  Well, there's how it could be done ^^^^^ not much needed.
